input.prompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest v3 project uses [ExpectedException] on several test methods.
After upgrading to MSTest v4, these no longer compile. What's the replacement?

data.reasoningText: The user is asking about migrating from MSTest v3 to MSTest v4, specifically about the `[ExpectedException]` attribute which has been removed. Let me look at their test file to understand what they have and provide the correct replacement.

data.content:
MSTest v4 removed `[ExpectedException]`. The replacement is `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>()` (or `Assert.ThrowsExactlyAsync<T>()` for async methods).

Here's the fix:

toolCalls.name: edit

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Build the project to verify changes

data.content:
Build succeeds. Here's a summary of the change:

| MSTest v3 | MSTest v4 |
|---|---|
| `[ExpectedException(typeof(T))]` | `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>(() => ...)` |

`ThrowsExactly<T>` is preferred because it scopes the assertion to the exact call you expect to throw, rather than any line in the method. For async methods, use `Assert.ThrowsExactlyAsync<T>`. If you also want to match derived exception types, use `Assert.Throws<T>` instead.
